claims.statement: Meta's global operations have been supported by 100% renewable energy since 2020, including all of our data centers.
claims.source_title: Meta Sustainability — Renewable energy

claims.statement: We are committed to restoring more water than we consume by 2030, focusing our efforts in regions of high and medium water stress.
claims.source_title: Meta Sustainability — Water

delivered.summary: Meta reports 60+ active water-restoration projects across 12 watersheds, projected to restore ~6B gallons/year at full implementation (1.6B gallons in 2024). On track but not yet at the stated 'restoring more than we consume' threshold; pace will need to accelerate as AI campuses (El Paso, Lebanon, Tulsa, Beaver Dam) come online.
delivered.source_title: Meta Data Centers — Restoring Water in Our Data Center Communities (Apr 2026)

claims.statement: Through our Community Action Grants program, we provide funding to local nonprofits and schools in our data center communities to support science, technology, engineering and math (STEM) education and community priorities.
claims.source_title: Meta Data Centers — Community

claims.statement: Meta has awarded millions in STEM education grants to schools and nonprofits in our data center host communities.
claims.source_title: Meta Data Centers — Community

claims.statement: We engage early with local stakeholders — elected officials, residents, and utility providers — to identify shared priorities and develop our data centers as long-term community partners.
claims.source_title: Meta Data Centers — Community engagement

claims.statement: Our goal is to operate on 24/7 carbon-free energy on every grid where we operate by 2030.
claims.source_title: Google Sustainability — 24/7 carbon-free energy

claims.statement: By 2030, we aim to replenish 120% of the freshwater we consume across our offices and data centers.
claims.source_title: Google Sustainability — Water stewardship

delivered.summary: Google continues to expand water-replenishment partnerships (Chattahoochee Trust for Public Land at LaGrange; regenerative-agriculture program in Oklahoma); however reported emissions grew 48% year-over-year as AI demand scaled, casting doubt on whether the 120% 2030 target's underlying intensity assumptions hold.
delivered.source_title: WRIC 8News — Google's 48% YoY emissions increase referenced in Chesterfield coverage

claims.statement: Google publishes annual economic impact reports for each of our U.S. data center communities, detailing investment, jobs supported, and local tax contributions.
claims.source_title: Google Data Centers — Community impact

claims.statement: Through our Data Center Grants program, Google.org has awarded grants to local nonprofits in our data center communities, focused on STEM education, workforce development, and digital inclusion.
claims.source_title: Google Data Centers — Grants

claims.statement: We invest in STEM education programs, including Code Next labs and partnerships with local community colleges to build pipelines into data center operations roles.
claims.source_title: Google Data Centers — Education

claims.statement: Our Datacenter Community Pledge commits us to advancing community prosperity, addressing local environmental impacts, building skills for jobs of today and the AI economy of tomorrow, and operating in a manner deserving of trust.
claims.source_title: Microsoft Datacenter Community Pledge (May 2024)

claims.statement: By 2030, Microsoft will be water positive — replenishing more water than we consume across our global operations.
claims.source_title: Microsoft Sustainability — Water positive by 2030

delivered.summary: Microsoft reports continued investment in water-replenishment projects, but Bloomberg reported May 6 2026 that the hourly-matched 2030 clean-power target is under internal review as AI growth strains 100/100/0 commitments; water-positive 2030 target remains official but contested internally.
delivered.source_title: Bloomberg — Microsoft clean-power target on chopping block over data-center boom (May 6 2026)

claims.statement: By 2030 Microsoft will be carbon negative, and by 2050 we will remove from the environment all the carbon the company has emitted either directly or by electrical consumption since it was founded in 1975.

claims.statement: We will partner with local educational institutions and nonprofits to build skills training programs aligned with the AI economy in the communities that host our datacenters.

claims.statement: Amazon matched 100% of the electricity consumed by our global operations with renewable energy in 2023, seven years ahead of our original 2030 target.

delivered.summary: xAI's local tax contribution to date is real but the projected '$100M+' figure has not been independently verified; Greater Memphis Chamber framing is forward-looking. Anthropic's May 6 2026 rental of 100% of Colossus 1 (~300 MW / 220K+ GPUs) may shift the tax-revenue trajectory but no updated public commitment has been published.
delivered.source_title: Tom's Hardware — Anthropic rents 100% of Colossus 1 compute (May 6 2026)

claims.statement: xAI will commit to build a $80 million wastewater recycling facility ... ensure that up to 4.7 billion gallons of water remain in the aquifer yearly.

delivered.summary: xAI publicly paused construction of the $80M water-recycling plant in April 2026 to prioritize finishing Colossus 2; both xAI and Elon Musk later publicly recommitted to the project but no construction restart has been confirmed as of May 2026. Memphis Mayor Paul Young continues to press for completion.
delivered.source_title: Action News 5 — xAI pauses then recommits to Memphis water-recycling plant (Apr 9 2026)

delivered.summary: May 2026 reporting found QTS's Fayetteville GA campus drew approximately 29 million gallons through two unmetered industrial-scale water connections over 15 months, contradicting the 'no water for cooling' design claim at that specific site. QTS paid ~$147K in retroactive charges; Fayette County declined to fine the company.
delivered.source_title: The Citizen — Behind Fayette's QTS Water Controversy (29M unmetered gallons, May 11 2026)

delivered.summary: NAACP / SELC / Earthjustice litigation (filed Apr 15 2026) alleges xAI operated 27 — and later up to 46 — unpermitted methane gas turbines at Memphis-area facilities, including at the new Southaven MS site; xAI's pledge to take no grid power during emergencies sits alongside an on-site fossil-generation footprint that bypassed Clean Air Act permitting per the suit.
delivered.source_title: Earthjustice — NAACP/SELC Clean Air Act suit against xAI Memphis/Southaven turbines

claims.statement: We've made a commitment nationally that we will not rely on local abatements or incentives.

delivered.summary: Microsoft's national 'no local abatements or incentives' commitment (Rima Alaily, Apr 14 2026) coexists with site-by-site PILOT and TIF arrangements (Mt Pleasant WI village-board approvals; Goodyear AZ data-center sales-tax exemptions). The framework distinguishes 'abatements' from 'incentives'; outside observers contest whether the practical effect differs.
delivered.source_title: The Assembly NC — Person County Microsoft NDA + incentive arrangements coverage

claims.statement: Since the development of our first datacenter in 2012, Microsoft has been working to strengthen, not strain, the community of Cheyenne.

claims.statement: These data centers are designed to not use water. All of the data centers that we're building (in) this part of Stargate are designed to not use water. The reason we do that is because it turns out that's harmful for the environment and this is a better solution.

claims.statement: Enough power for one million four-bedroom homes in the United States.

claims.statement: We were proud to cheer on the Greater Manassas Baseball League team in the World Series last year and were excited to support them again this year. We value our relationships with the Northern Virginia community and were pleased to be able to help these players and their families make the trip.
claims.source_title: QTS — Dave Murray (SVP Community Impact) on Greater Manassas Baseball League sponsorship

claims.statement: This is the perfect home for Meta for several reasons, including great local talent, strong community partnerships, and reliable infrastructure. We are excited to become part of the Beaver Dam community, and we look forward to a strong partnership with the city and the state for years to come.
claims.source_title: WEDC — Brad Davis (Meta Director of Data Center Community and Economic Development) on Beaver Dam

claims.statement: In Beaver Dam we will restore 100% of the water consumed in our data center operations to local watersheds.
claims.source_title: Daily Dodge — Brad Davis (Meta) on Beaver Dam water restoration

claims.statement: Microsoft is committed to being a good neighbor and active community member anywhere we build, own, or operate facilities. We look forward to sharing more detailed plans with the community as we have them and move through the years-long development process.
claims.source_title: The Assembly NC — Microsoft spokesperson on Person County data center

claims.statement: While [Microsoft is] at the earliest stage of the development process, in any future datacenter project, the community can expect that we will: pay our way to ensure our development doesn't increase residents' electricity prices, minimize our water use and replenish more water than we use, create jobs for community residents, add to the tax base, and strengthen the community by investing in local IT training and nonprofits.
claims.source_title: The Assembly NC — Microsoft spokesperson on Person County five commitments

claims.statement: We have a water-positive goal. In Clinton, we will actually use no water.
claims.source_title: SuperTalk MS — Roger Wehner (AWS VP Economic Development) on Clinton water use

claims.statement: A super unique opportunity to take a pre-existing building that needed a little love — we're retrofitting that building and reinventing it as part of this broader story that we're creating here in Mississippi.
claims.source_title: SuperTalk MS — Wehner (AWS) on Clinton Delphi retrofit

claims.statement: We are excited to move forward with this updated energy solution, which reflects our commitment to both the latest innovation and community priorities as we advance the next generation of AI infrastructure.
claims.source_title: PR Newswire — Mahesh Thiagarajan (Oracle EVP OCI) on Project Jupiter fuel-cell pivot

claims.statement: Project Jupiter started with a belief that Doña Ana County could become a Tier 1 industrial engine for New Mexico. We said we could help bring cleaner energy, stronger infrastructure, more jobs and new investment to southern New Mexico, and that vision is becoming reality. With this announcement, Project Jupiter is becoming a platform for better jobs, stronger infrastructure, and generational opportunity in a region with the talent, work ethic, and ambition to help lead New Mexico's next chapter of growth.
claims.source_title: PR Newswire — Lanham Napier (Chairman, BorderPlex Digital Assets — Oracle JV partner) on Project Jupiter

claims.statement: When we go into a community and spend time and energy and build relationships, it sometimes culminates in cool events like this. We do not want this to be a burden to the community. All the sections that we have that are adjacent to our properties, we will continue to maintain. This is on our property that we have donated to the project that ultimately allows people to enjoy this path for many, many years to come. The Fayetteville project has been one of the most successful projects that we've had.
claims.source_title: The Citizen — Tag Greason (QTS co-CEO) on Fayetteville community partnership

claims.statement: Our mission statement is to love each other, serve our communities and connect the world. It is a privilege to be here, be part of this community. We hope that this serves as one of many great moments in the future to come.
claims.source_title: The Citizen — David Robey (QTS co-CEO) on Fayetteville community

claims.statement: QTS is pleased to announce our expansion in York County, SC with a new campus that supports growth for QTS and its partners. Our investment in York County is consistent with QTS' strategy of securing access to infrastructure at scale in strategic markets to support accelerating demand from our hyperscale, enterprise, and government customer base. We look forward to partnering with the community along with local and state leadership, further enhancing South Carolina as a leading technology center.
claims.source_title: SC Governor's Office — Chad Williams (QTS CEO) on York County data center

claims.statement: Building an American AI factory that can scale to 10 gigawatts of capacity illustrates Crusoe's commitment to delivering infrastructure at the scale needed for the U.S. to win the global AI race.
claims.source_title: Tallgrass newsroom — Chase Lochmiller (Crusoe co-founder & CEO) on Wyoming AI data center

claims.statement: We don't just build data centers; we become part of the community. That's why we're committed to a sustainable, long-term presence that benefits the entire community. This includes paying for 100% of the cost of power for the site and any infrastructure needs driven by our operations.
claims.source_title: WSBT 22 — Google statement on Project Maize, Michigan City Indiana

claims.statement: Google is considering a potential data center project in Scioto County, Ohio. We are still very early in the process, working closely with the local leadership.
claims.source_title: Scioto County Daily News — Molly Kocour Boyle (Google Head of Midwest DC Public Affairs) on Franklin Furnace

claims.statement: Partnering with SB Energy brings together their strength in data center infrastructure and energy development, and OpenAI's deep domain expertise in data center engineering. The result is a fast, reliable way to scale compute through large, highly optimized AI data centers.
claims.source_title: OpenAI — Greg Brockman (President) on SB Energy / Milam County 1.2 GW site

claims.statement: Oracle recognizes that when we enter a community, we have an obligation to be a good citizen.
claims.source_title: Oracle blog — AI Infrastructure in 2026 and Our Commitment to Local Communities (good-citizen commitment)

claims.statement: North Dakota has been very welcoming to us. The state's energy industry, government and communities have embraced Crusoe as a solution to the challenging problem of natural gas flaring. North Dakota really gave Crusoe our start, and we want to give back in ways that align with our values of innovation, environmental stewardship and personal growth. This is why we are donating to STEM education programs and community building activities like the Maah Daah Hey run.
claims.source_title: Crusoe newsroom — Cully Cavness (Crusoe President) on North Dakota community giving

claims.statement: At Meta, we're building the infrastructure that will power the next generation of AI technology, and Lebanon is now at the heart of that effort. This is one of our largest investments to date, and it marks an important milestone in our AI ambitions.
claims.source_title: Commercial Observer — Rachel Peterson (Meta VP Data Centers) on Lebanon IN groundbreaking

claims.statement: We've been proud to call Indiana home since 2024, and this project deepens that commitment, creating local jobs, supporting the community, and partnering with state and local leaders to drive real impact across the region.
claims.source_title: City of Lebanon — Rachel Peterson (Meta VP Data Centers) on Lebanon IN announcement

claims.statement: This is our 27th data center here in the U.S. … a tenet of our data center program from day one has been to play a really strong, positive role in the communities that we're in.
claims.source_title: WFYI — Rachel Peterson (Meta) on community engagement framing at Lebanon

claims.statement: Data centers and this infrastructure is critical to everything we do on the internet. You think of the cloud, those are data centers.
claims.source_title: El Paso Matters — Brad Davis (Meta Director Data Center Community & Economic Development) on El Paso campus

claims.statement: And when you have a global platform like ours and intentions of deploying that AI everywhere, it's going to require very large amounts of infrastructure similar to what we're talking about here in El Paso.
claims.source_title: El Paso Matters — Brad Davis (Meta) on El Paso AI infrastructure scale

claims.statement: We have 1,500 construction workers on site today. We are getting ready to pour foundations. We are moving forward.
claims.source_title: El Paso Matters — Holli Davies (Meta Community Development Manager) on El Paso construction

claims.statement: With today's announcement, Google is deepening our roots in Virginia [and] extending our investments across the state to help position Virginia — and America — for the opportunities technology can deliver. Google's investments in technical infrastructure and AI skills development help to ensure that people across Virginia and across the United States have access to opportunity in this exciting era of American innovation.
claims.source_title: Virginia Business — Ruth Porat (Alphabet/Google President & CIO) on Chesterfield announcement

claims.statement: Too often, as the governor has said to me, when people think of the digital economy, they only think of places like Northern Virginia or other tech hubs. And so today, all of us are demonstrating that cutting-edge technology comprises every corner of the commonwealth and every corner of the country.
claims.source_title: The Richmonder — Ruth Porat (Alphabet/Google President) on Chesterfield siting decision

claims.statement: We're excited to share that Google has purchased land in Putnam County, West Virginia, for a new data center campus. We are early in the process, and substantial work remains, but we are appreciative of the strong business environment created by Governor Morrisey to provide a pathway for projects of this scale. We look forward to continued partnership with the Governor and local leaders in Putnam County on this opportunity here in West Virginia.
claims.source_title: WV Governor's Office — Clay Allsop (Google Regional Head of Data Center Public Affairs) on Putnam County

claims.statement: AI can only fulfill its promise if we build the compute to power it. That compute is the key to ensuring everyone can benefit from AI and to unlocking future breakthroughs. We're already making historic progress toward that goal through Stargate and moving quickly not just to meet its initial commitment, but to lay the foundation for what comes next.
claims.source_title: Vantage Data Centers — Sam Altman (OpenAI CEO) on Wisconsin Stargate Lighthouse announcement

claims.statement: Expanding Stargate to Wisconsin is another major step toward building the infrastructure that will help ensure everyone can benefit from AI.
claims.source_title: Vantage Data Centers — Peter Hoeschele (OpenAI VP Industrial Compute) on Wisconsin Stargate

claims.statement: Amazon's multi-billion-dollar investment in Pennsylvania reinforces our dedication to advancing AI innovation while creating lasting economic opportunities in the state.
claims.source_title: Falls Township — David Zapolsky (Amazon Chief Global Affairs & Legal Officer) on Falls Twp campus

claims.statement: Amazon's $10 billion investment in North Carolina underscores our commitment to driving innovation and advancing the future of cloud computing and AI technologies.
claims.source_title: NC Governor's Office — David Zapolsky (Amazon) on Richmond County NC

claims.statement: This investment will position North Carolina as a hub for cutting-edge technology, create hundreds of high-skilled jobs, and drive significant economic growth. We look forward to partnering with state and local leaders, local suppliers, and educational institutions to nurture the next generation of talent.
claims.source_title: NC Governor's Office — David Zapolsky (Amazon) on Richmond County NC workforce + education partnerships

claims.statement: Amazon's $12 billion investment in northwest Louisiana will build next-generation data center campuses to support AI and cloud computing, ensuring opportunities for local communities. We're creating hundreds of high-paying jobs and making substantial investments in local infrastructure.
claims.source_title: Louisiana Economic Development — David Zapolsky (Amazon) on northwest LA campuses

claims.statement: We have very deep commitments about water and energy. And we live up to those. We have high goals and we exceed them regularly. And we're 80% more efficient with water than the industry average.
claims.source_title: KSLA — Roger Wehner (AWS Director Economic Development) on Caddo/Bossier water + energy efficiency

claims.statement: The thing I'm most passionate about, quite honestly, is we love the fact that we go someplace, we immediately invest in the schools.
claims.source_title: KSLA — Roger Wehner (AWS) on Caddo/Bossier school investment

claims.statement: We don't want to pass cost on anything.
claims.source_title: KSLA — Roger Wehner (AWS Director Economic Development) on Bossier/Caddo infrastructure costs

claims.statement: Amazon's $3 billion investment in Warren County will create lasting opportunities for Mississippi families and communities, building on our more-than-a-decade-long partnership with the Magnolia State. We're investing in the people and communities that make Mississippi strong, from training more than 6,500 Mississippians through our workforce development programs to launching our new Warren County Community Fund that will address community needs like STEM education. This is what responsible growth looks like—bringing cutting-edge technology infrastructure to America while ensuring local communities benefit directly from that investment.
claims.source_title: Amazon Newsroom — David Zapolsky on Warren County campus + Community Fund

claims.statement: xAI is scaling at an immeasurable pace — we are building our third massive data center in the greater Memphis area. MACROHARDRR pushes our Colossus training compute to ~2GW – by far the most powerful AI system on Earth. This is insane execution speed by xAI and the state of Mississippi. We are grateful to Governor Reeves for his support of building xAI at warp speed.
claims.source_title: MS Governor's Office — Elon Musk (xAI CEO) on Southaven MACROHARD/Colossus 3 announcement

claims.statement: Approving this permit today will unlock the power needed for the next era of AI innovation in America, while also securing lasting economic growth, job creation and technology leadership in Mississippi.
claims.source_title: Mississippi Today — Brent Mayo (xAI VP Operations) at MS Environmental Quality Permit Board hearing

claims.statement: xAI believes in always doing right when no one is looking.
claims.source_title: Mississippi Today — Brent Mayo (xAI VP Operations) at Southaven permit hearing

claims.statement: We look forward to expanding Google's investments in Missouri and helping Missourians benefit from the next wave of American innovation. To deliver the upside of technology, we are investing in workforce development and energy affordability, both directly and through our partnerships with local organizations. This commitment will provide thousands of Missourians with valuable technical and trade skills, while supporting energy affordability for residents in Montgomery County and across the state.
claims.source_title: Google Deepens Missouri Roots — Ruth Porat on the Missouri investment (PR Newswire)

claims.statement: To further address energy affordability, Google today also announced a $20 million Energy Impact Fund to support programs that drive down monthly energy bills for households in Missouri—focused on Montgomery, Clay, Platte, and other counties surrounding Google's planned data centers in Kansas City and New Florence.
claims.source_title: Google Deepens Missouri Roots with New Data Center, Energy, and Community Investments (PR Newswire)

claims.statement: To date, Google has contracted to bring more than one gigawatt (GW) of new generation capacity to Missouri, and—through its partnership with Ameren—Google is supporting the development of more than 500 megawatts (MW) of additional capacity.
claims.source_title: Google Deepens Missouri Roots with New Data Center, Energy, and Community Investments (PR Newswire)

claims.statement: The Montgomery County data center will use advanced air-cooling technology, limiting water consumption to uses like kitchens.
claims.source_title: Google Deepens Missouri Roots with New Data Center, Energy, and Community Investments (PR Newswire)

claims.statement: This project will enable the center to train more than 2,300 construction laborers, including 1,500 apprentices, over the next two years.
claims.source_title: Google Deepens Missouri Roots with New Data Center, Energy, and Community Investments (PR Newswire)

claims.statement: When Google builds data centers, the company pays for 100% of the power it uses, and it will cover the infrastructure costs directly driven by its operations.
claims.source_title: Google Deepens Missouri Roots with New Data Center, Energy, and Community Investments (PR Newswire)

claims.statement: Our conversations with Coweta County followed standard, transparent processes that are open to all stakeholders. Prologis's involvement was consistent with customary public engagement in land-use and policy discussions.
claims.source_title: DeSmog — Prologis spokesperson on Project Sail engagement (Dec 2025)

delivered.summary: Prologis described its Coweta County engagement as 'standard, transparent processes,' but DeSmog's December 2025 investigation documented that the county's data-center rules were rewritten in closed-door working sessions before public hearings on Project Sail, and Citizens for Rural Coweta filed a 17-plaintiff lawsuit in May 2026 alleging due-process and zoning violations during the rezoning. The contested status reflects that both descriptions of the same process appear on the public record.
delivered.source_title: DeSmog — Behind Closed Doors, Georgia County Rewrote Data Center Rules (Dec 2025)

claims.statement: A lot of communities don't have any sort of codes or regulations around how these are being constructed. They're doing the right thing by spending the time and research to make sure that (data centers) are brought into their code in a prudent manner.
claims.source_title: AJC — JC Witt (Prologis SVP Data Center Investments) on Coweta County's data-center code work

claims.statement: Nine hundred megawatts is nothing to take lightly. It's something that we really want to execute on for the community and for the company.
claims.source_title: AJC — JC Witt (Prologis SVP Data Center Investments) on Project Sail

claims.statement: What we like, as a company with a conscience, is that there's already this massive Georgia Power generating station and the lines are there.
claims.source_title: AJC — Kent Mason (Prologis Atlanta market officer) on Project Sail siting

delivered.summary: Prologis framed the Plant Yates siting as relying on transmission infrastructure that's 'already there,' but Georgia Power has confirmed the load actually requires the new Ashley Park-Wansley 500 kV line — a ~35-mile build for which the utility expects 'roughly 20 to 30 homes' to be removed, with documented eminent-domain disputes in Fayette and Coweta counties (the Maszk family, Ansley Brown) as of May 2026. Both can be true: existing infrastructure proximity was a real siting factor; the actual load also requires new construction with material residential displacement.
delivered.source_title: AJC — Georgia Power's data center build-out raises fears of eminent domain (May 2026)

claims.statement: We've agreed to finance the entire 2.7 gigawatts of new power supply through an agreement with DTE to help ensure any costs associated with our data center project come to us (Google).
claims.source_title: Michigan Advance — Google and DTE: Van Buren Township data center agreement and contested case hearing

claims.statement: Google in Ohio will pay 100% of the electric required to run the campus along with paying AEP to provide infrastructure locally.
claims.source_title: WSAZ — Google hosts Scioto County data center information fair (May 2026)

claims.statement: We pay the full costs for energy used by our data centers and work closely with utilities to plan for our energy needs years in advance to ensure residents aren't negatively impacted.
claims.source_title: Meta Data Centers — Hello, Lebanon! (Feb 2026)

claims.statement: Amazon has worked with the local utility, Southwestern Electric Power Company (SWEPCO), to ensure we pay 100% of the costs associated with our new data center campus in Louisiana. This includes covering all expenses for new energy infrastructure and upgrades required to serve the data centers.
claims.source_title: Amazon — Caddo and Bossier parishes Louisiana data center announcement (Feb 2026)

claims.statement: Amazon has worked with the local utility, Southwestern Electric Power Company (SWEPCO), to ensure we pay 100% of the costs associated with our new data center campus in Louisiana. This includes covering all expenses for new energy infrastructure and upgrades required to serve the data centers.
claims.source_title: Amazon — Caddo and Bossier parishes Louisiana data center announcement (Feb 2026)

claims.statement: Amazon has worked with Entergy Mississippi to ensure we pay 100% of the costs associated with our new data center campuses, covering all expenses for new energy infrastructure and upgrades.
claims.source_title: Amazon — $25 billion Mississippi data centers announcement (Apr 2026)

claims.statement: Amazon has worked with Entergy Mississippi to ensure we pay 100% of the costs associated with our new data center campuses, covering all expenses for new energy infrastructure and upgrades.
claims.source_title: Amazon — $25 billion Mississippi data centers announcement (Apr 2026)

claims.statement: Amazon has worked with Entergy Mississippi to ensure we pay 100% of the costs associated with our new data center campuses, covering all expenses for new energy infrastructure and upgrades.
claims.source_title: Amazon — $25 billion Mississippi data centers announcement (Apr 2026)

claims.statement: Google will cover 100% of the costs for new grid infrastructure required to serve the facility. That protects Minnesota ratepayers.
claims.source_title: Google Blog — Pine Island, Minnesota data center announcement

claims.statement: Google and Minnesota Power are partnering to bring 700 megawatts of new clean energy — including 300 megawatts of wind and 400 megawatts of battery storage — to the Arrowhead region, with no cost increases to existing Minnesota Power customers.
claims.source_title: Data Center Dynamics — Google confirms 403-acre Hermantown, Minnesota campus

claims.statement: The Stargate Michigan data center will create more than 2,500 union construction jobs and 450 permanent on-site jobs, with an additional 1,500 jobs expected across Washtenaw County.
claims.source_title: OpenAI — Stargate Michigan groundbreaking

claims.statement: The Stargate Michigan project includes a $10 million investment in the Saline Recreation Center, a community benefit commitment made by all consortium partners at the June 2026 groundbreaking.
claims.source_title: OpenAI — Stargate Michigan groundbreaking

claims.statement: The Stargate Michigan campus will operate at 1,400 megawatts of DTE Energy-served capacity, using 100% existing DTE resources supplemented with new battery storage to power the three-building data center campus.
claims.source_title: OpenAI — Stargate Michigan groundbreaking

claims.statement: Microsoft will achieve a 40 percent improvement in datacenter water-use intensity by 2030.
claims.source_title: Microsoft — Building Community-First AI Infrastructure (Brad Smith, Jan 13 2026)

claims.statement: Microsoft is funding more than $25 million of water and sewer improvements near its Leesburg, Virginia datacenter.
claims.source_title: Microsoft — Building Community-First AI Infrastructure (Brad Smith, Jan 13 2026)

claims.statement: We operate with a focus on being good stewards in the community through ensuring we're paying our costs for energy, adding new energy to grids, minimizing water use, preserving local habitats and supporting water restoration projects.
claims.source_title: Meta Data Centers — datacenters.atmeta.com

claims.statement: Crusoe uses GE Vernova natural gas turbines with SCR technology for the Abilene campus power plant, with approximately 90% lower emissions than traditional gas turbine alternatives.
claims.source_title: Crusoe — An inside look at the Abilene AI data center (Aug 2025)
